page: Microsoft Azure D128as_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dasv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with 64 physical cores and 128 vCPUs via hyperthreading. The dedicated CPU operates at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz and includes cache sizes of 48 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 32 MB L3. The system is equipped with 512.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU.

This instance does not include local NVMe storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met using external Premium Storage options, for which the instance is capable. Additionally, the configuration lacks integrated GPU hardware and does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark results place the D128as_v7 in the top tier for both single-core and multi-core CPU performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 232,585.66 and a PassMark CPU score of 134,134.92. Memory bandwidth is also highly rated, reaching 12,238.78 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 386.84 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. The processor's support for advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512, AVX_VNNI, and cryptography standards like AES and SHA_NI, enables top-tier performance in database operations, static web serving, and LLM inference. For instance, it achieves 178.44 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and processes 331.25 tokens per second for medium 7B LLM prompt processing.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D128as_v7 offers high resource density and top-tier compute performance per resource unit within the general-purpose hardware class. It is suited for large-scale general-purpose workloads, multi-threaded database operations, static web serving, CPU-based machine learning inference, and heavy compression or decompression tasks.
description: The Microsoft Azure D128as_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dasv7 family, built on the x86_64 architecture and powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor. It features 128 vCPUs across 64 physical cores running at 4.5 GHz, paired with 512.0 GB of system memory. This configuration lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying instead on external Premium Storage. Benchmarks place this instance in the top tier for both single-core and multi-core CPU performance, as well as memory bandwidth and database operations. The processor supports advanced vector and cryptographic instructions, enabling high-throughput tasks such as Redis operations, static web serving, and CPU-based LLM inference. The D128as_v7 is suited for enterprise-grade general-purpose applications, multi-threaded database workloads, and high-volume web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D128as_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 128 vCPUs, an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz, and 512 GB of RAM, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D128as_v7 is a general-purpose VM with 128 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and a 4.5 GHz AMD EPYC CPU, delivering top-tier compute performance.
tagline: A high-density, general-purpose virtual machine delivering top-tier AMD EPYC compute performance and balanced memory for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a dedicated 128-vCPU AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz
Equipped with 512.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB per vCPU
Delivers top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database performance
Lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, requiring external Premium Storage
Optimized for enterprise databases, static web serving, and CPU-based LLM inference
